locale.label: Language
release.update_available_body: This client is on {current}, while the server is already on {latest}. Refresh or update the app when you can.
hero.title: Run the household like a cheerful little mastermind.
hero.lede: Sign in to the live TaskBandit server to review chore submissions, manage settings, and keep the whole household moving.
hero.mascot: Bandit is waiting at the door.
hero.authenticated_title: The raccoon crew is live and ready for chores.
hero.authenticated_lede: This dashboard now talks to the real API, so approvals, live chores, and household settings reflect the current server state.
hero.login_admin_lede: Sign in to manage household settings, templates, and approvals.
hero.login_admin_mascot: Bandit is guarding the admin desk.
hero.login_client_lede: Sign in to see your chores and keep the client ready for quick, app-like access.
hero.login_client_mascot: Bandit is ready for the next home-screen shortcut.
hero.mascot_alt: TaskBandit raccoon mascot with a chore clipboard
workspace.admin_only_body: This entrypoint is reserved for admin accounts. Use the client workspace for everyday household work.
pwa.install_body: Add the client workspace to your home screen for a cleaner, app-like household view.
pwa.install_success: TaskBandit was added to the device or is ready to open as an installed app.
pwa.browser_notifications_body: Turn on browser notifications so the client workspace can alert you even when it is in the background.
pwa.browser_notifications_enabled: Browser notifications are now enabled for this device.
pwa.browser_notifications_failed: TaskBandit could not enable browser notifications on this device.
pwa.browser_notifications_denied: Browser notifications are blocked for this site. Update the browser permission if you want to enable them later.
page.overview_description: Start from the household pulse, approvals, and the quickest routes into daily operations.
page.chores_description: Work through personal chores, then switch to the household board, calendar, or approvals when needed.
page.templates_description: Build chore templates, tune recurrence and proof rules, and schedule live work from one place.
page.household_description: Manage who is in the household, how they sign in, and how recovery access is kept available.
page.notifications_description: Watch the inbox, device coverage, and delivery readiness with push-first visibility.
page.settings_description: Control household rules, auth, OIDC, SMTP, and the safety switches that keep the instance recoverable.
page.admin_description: Use the admin room for system status, backups, notification health, and delivery recovery tools.
page.logs_description: Review the household audit trail and runtime logs when you need operational detail or troubleshooting evidence.
onboarding.title: First-Time Setup
onboarding.welcome_body: This quick guided setup helps you turn TaskBandit from a fresh install into a live household system without guessing where to start.
onboarding.settings_body: Review visibility, auth, OIDC, SMTP, notifications, and penalty settings so your household starts with the right guardrails.
onboarding.members_body: Add parents and children so assignments, approvals, leaderboards, and permissions reflect your real household.
onboarding.chores_body: Create chore templates with recurrence, proof rules, and checklists so TaskBandit can start generating meaningful work right away.
onboarding.overview_body: Once the first template and members are in place, schedule chores, review submissions, watch notifications, and let the raccoon crew take over the daily rhythm.
onboarding.completed: Onboarding marked complete. You can still change everything from the dashboard.
onboarding.complete_failed: TaskBandit could not complete the onboarding flow.
panel.providers_hint: Local auth is ready. OIDC is optional.
overview.quick_actions_hint: Open the page you want instead of digging through one giant dashboard.
auth.login_failed: TaskBandit could not sign you in.
auth.password_reset_request_hint: TaskBandit will send a reset link if the address belongs to a local account and SMTP is ready.
auth.password_reset_request_failed: TaskBandit could not send the password reset email.
auth.password_reset_complete_failed: TaskBandit could not reset the password.
auth.password_reset_token_ready: Reset link accepted. Choose a new password below.
auth.signup_failed: TaskBandit could not create the account.
auth.signup_success: Parent account created and signed in.
auth.session_expired: Your session expired. Please sign in again.
auth.setup_load_failed: TaskBandit could not load the household access setup. Please retry.
auth.local_disabled_notice: Local sign-in is disabled right now for this household.
auth.local_forced_note: Local sign-in is being force-enabled from config as a recovery path.
auth.oidc_success: Signed in through OIDC and dashboard refreshed.
auth.oidc_hint: Use your external household identity provider if local sign-in is not the right fit.
auth.demo_ready: The seeded demo household is ready to use.
bootstrap.title: Create the first household
bootstrap.step_templates_helper: Pick one group at a time
bootstrap.account_hint: Create the household and first admin account first. We will choose starter routines in the next step.
bootstrap.self_signup_hint: Leave this off unless you want other parent accounts to be able to join themselves after setup.
bootstrap.starter_templates_hint: Pick the routines you want TaskBandit to preload for this household. You can edit or remove them later.
bootstrap.active_group_hint: Only this group is shown here, so setup stays focused.
bootstrap.no_starter_templates: No starter templates are available right now.
bootstrap.review_title: Check the setup before creating it
bootstrap.review_hint: Nothing is created until you press the final create button.
bootstrap.review_self_signup_enabled: Parent self-signup will be enabled after setup.
bootstrap.review_self_signup_disabled: Parent self-signup will stay disabled after setup.
bootstrap.created: Household created and owner signed in.
bootstrap.create_failed: TaskBandit could not create the first household.
attachments.open_failed: TaskBandit could not open the proof image.
approval.empty: No chores are waiting for approval right now.
approval.failed: The review action could not be completed.
takeover.panel_hint: Handle reassignment requests here so chores move cleanly between household members.
takeover.requested_by: {name} asked you to take over this chore.
takeover.approved_notice: Takeover request approved and the chore was reassigned.
takeover.action_failed: TaskBandit could not process the takeover request.
celebration.title: Nice work!
celebration.phrase_3: That chore never saw you coming.
audit.empty: No audit activity has been recorded yet.
logs.empty: No runtime log entries have been captured yet.
logs.export_failed: TaskBandit could not export the runtime logs.
logs.load_failed: TaskBandit could not load the runtime logs.
system_status.hint: This runtime check helps admins confirm that the self-hosted instance is actually ready: app reachability, database, storage, auth recovery path, push delivery, and email fallback.
system_status.load_failed: TaskBandit could not load the system status.
system_status.empty: No system status is available yet.
backup.hint: This panel shows what to preserve before migrating the stack: the host-mounted data root, the compose and env files, and the exports that help with support or manual recovery.
backup.load_failed: TaskBandit could not load the backup readiness view.
backup.empty: No backup readiness data is available yet.
backup.server_paths_hint: These are container-side paths. For host migration, preserve the bind-mounted host paths shown in the first card.
backup.path_unavailable: Not exposed to the server runtime
backup.checklist_hint: Use this as the minimum handoff list before you move or recover the stack.
backup.checklist_paths_body: Keep the data root, Postgres data, compose file, and .env together. The household snapshot alone is not a full stack backup.
backup.checklist_exports_body: Pull a household snapshot and runtime logs before a migration so you have user-facing data and diagnostics available if the move goes sideways.
backup.checklist_recovery_body: Local admin recovery accounts: {localAdmins}. Recovery email routes ready: {resetEmails}. Keep at least one local admin password or force local auth from config before relying only on OIDC.
backup.checklist_cutover_body: After cutover, open System Status and verify app reachability, database access, storage writes, auth, push, and email before calling the migration done.
backup.exports_hint: These exports help with support, audits, and manual recovery checks, but they do not replace the mounted host data.
backup.recovery_hint: Recovery should stay understandable even if OIDC or a device platform breaks. Keep one path that works without the preferred provider.
notification_recovery.hint: If a push or email fallback fails, admins can retry it here without leaving the web UI.
notification_recovery.empty: There are no failed notification deliveries to recover right now.
notification_recovery.none_push: No failed push deliveries right now.
notification_recovery.none_email: No failed email fallbacks right now.
notification_recovery.push_retry_success: Push delivery retry queued and processed.
notification_recovery.email_retry_succes: Email fallback retry queued and processed.
notification_recovery.push_retry_failed: TaskBandit could not retry the failed push delivery.
notification_recovery.email_retry_failed: TaskBandit could not retry the failed email fallback.
points.empty: No points history has been recorded yet.
notifications.mark_failed: TaskBandit could not update the notification.
submission.empty: No currently actionable chores are assigned to you.
submission.priority_hint: Fix returned chores first, then finish what is already in progress.
submission.empty_in_progress: No chores are actively in progress right now.
submission.empty_ready_to_start: Nothing new is waiting to be started.
submission.one_tap: No checklist is required, so this becomes a one-tap submit.
submission.success: Chore submission sent to the server.
submission.failed: The chore submission could not be completed.
submission.complete_required: Complete all required checklist items before submitting.
submission.photo_hint_required: Add at least one proof photo before sending this chore.
submission.photo_hint_optional: You can attach proof photos here if you want extra context.
submission.photo_required_missing: Add at least one proof photo before submitting.
settings.full_visibility: Members can see full household chore details
settings.takeover_points_hint: Use a signed whole number. Positive values reward the person who accepts the takeover. Negative values deduct points from the person who asks someone else to take it over. Zero disables this rule.
settings.takeover_points_reward: When someone accepts a takeover, they earn {points} extra points.
settings.takeover_points_penalty: When someone asks another member to take over a chore, {points} points are deducted from them.
settings.local_auth_forced_note: Local sign-in is still effectively on because TASKBANDIT_FORCE_LOCAL_AUTH_ENABLED is enabled in config.
settings.recovery_summary_hint: Treat this summary as the auth fallback check before changing login providers or moving the stack.
settings.recovery_local_admins_detail: {configured} of {total} admins can still sign in locally
settings.recovery_backup_callout: Admins with both local recovery and an email route: {emails}. Backup Readiness in the admin room shows the migration checklist and export shortcuts.
settings.recovery_member_password_hint: Saving or rotating an admin password here keeps a local recovery path available even if OIDC is the preferred sign-in flow.
settings.oidc_secret_saved: Secret already stored. Keep or replace it here.
settings.smtp_password_saved: Password already stored. Keep or replace it here.
settings.smtp_test_failed: TaskBandit could not verify the SMTP connection.
settings.notify_reviews: Notify me about approvals, fixes, and cancellations
settings.notify_due_soon: Notify me when chores are due soon
settings.notify_overdue: Notify me when chores are overdue or penalized
settings.notify_daily_summary: Send me the daily household summary
settings.push_primary_hint: TaskBandit treats push as the main household notification channel. Sign in on the Android app or enable notifications in the client web app to register this device for live reminders and review updates.
settings.household_notification_health_h: Admins can use this to see who has a push-ready phone, who would fall back to email, and who still has no delivery path configured.
settings.household_notification_health_e: No household delivery health records are available yet.
settings.push_household_disabled: Household-level push notifications are currently disabled by the admin settings.
settings.no_notification_devices: No push-enabled devices are registered for this account yet. Sign in on the Android app or enable notifications in the client web app to connect one.
settings.notification_device_remove_fail: TaskBandit could not remove the notification device.
settings.test_notification_sent: Sent a test notification to {name}. Push sent {pushSent}, failed {pushFailed}. Email sent {emailSent}, failed {emailFailed}, skipped {emailSkipped}.
settings.test_notification_failed: TaskBandit could not send the test notification.
settings.save_failed: TaskBandit could not save the household settings.
settings.notification_preferences_save_f: TaskBandit could not save the notification preferences.
settings.overdue_processed: Processed {count} overdue chores and applied {points} points in penalties.
settings.overdue_none: No overdue chores needed penalty processing.
settings.overdue_process_failed: TaskBandit could not process overdue penalties.
settings.notifications_processed: Generated {reminders} reminder notifications and {summaries} daily summaries. Push delivery sent {pushSent} and failed {pushFailed}. Email fallback sent {emailSent}, failed {emailFailed}, and skipped {emailSkipped}.
settings.notifications_process_failed: TaskBandit could not process notification maintenance.
members.password_generated_hint: A strong temporary password is generated automatically. You can keep it or change it before creating the member.
members.create_section_hint: Add a new household member here without mixing creation fields into the edit list.
members.created_invited: Household member created and invite email sent.
members.create_failed: TaskBandit could not create the household member.
members.update_failed: TaskBandit could not update the household member.
members.admin_role_locked: The first household owner keeps admin access. You can still update the profile, email, or password here.
members.password_adds_local_auth: Saving a password here will add local recovery sign-in for this member.
members.send_invite_email: Send welcome email with sign-in details
templates.title: Type
templates.translations_hint: Add optional translations for other app languages here. If a translation is missing, TaskBandit falls back to the default language.
templates.default_locale_live_hint: The default language is edited in the main fields above. Use the tabs for the additional languages.
templates.create_failed: TaskBandit could not create the chore template.
templates.update_failed: TaskBandit could not update the chore template.
templates.delete_failed: TaskBandit could not delete the chore template.
templates.search_placeholder: Search by group, type, or description
templates.search_empty: No templates match the current search.
templates.no_checklist_draft: Add checklist items if this chore needs a guided flow.
templates.follow_up_hint: Create another template first if this chore should trigger a follow-up.
templates.follow_up_group_required: Set the group first so TaskBandit can offer valid follow-up chores from the same group.
templates.follow_up_same_group_empty: Create another template in this group before adding a follow-up.
templates.follow_up_same_group_hint: Follow-up chores can only point to templates from the same group.
templates.subtypes_hint: Optional: define reusable subtypes here (for example "White clothes" or "Colour clothes"). When a subtype is selected, it carries through to follow-up chores too.
instances.subtype_required: Please select a subtype for this chore type.
instances.create_failed: TaskBandit could not create the chore instance.
instances.start_failed: TaskBandit could not start the chore.
instances.update_failed: TaskBandit could not update the chore instance.
instances.cancel_failed: TaskBandit could not cancel the chore.
instances.close_cycle_failed: TaskBandit could not cancel the repeating chore.
instances.close_cycle_confirm: Cancel this chore and stop its future repeats? This will cancel every still-open chore in the repeat.
instances.repeat_forever: Keep repeating until I cancel it
instances.repeat_after_occurrences: Stop after a number of repeats
instances.repeat_occurrences_required: Choose how many total occurrences this repeating chore should have.
instances.repeat_end_date_required: Choose the date when this repeating chore should stop.
exports.chores_failed: TaskBandit could not download the chore export.
exports.household_snapshot_failed: TaskBandit could not download the household snapshot.
chores.empty_assigned_elsewhere: No active chores are currently assigned to someone else.
chores.empty_unassigned: No active chores are currently unassigned.
empty.filtered_chores: No chores match the current filters.
error.load_dashboard: TaskBandit could not load the live dashboard.
error.missing_template: The chore template could not be found for this instance.
